title: Nova Lingua Function Record
description: The mandatory self-describing record for a function in the Nova Lingua commons. Every function is identified by the content-hash of the canonical serialization of this record. v0.1 — see spec/README.md for evolution policy and what is deliberately deferred.

schema_version.description: Schema version. Pinned to the record so old records remain readable as the schema evolves.

hash.description: Content-hash of this record's canonical serialization with the 'hash' field removed. The function's global identity.

name_hints.description: Surface names useful for human display and lexical discovery. Carry no semantic weight; the function's identity is its hash.

examples.description: Canonical worked input/output pairs. At least one is required — a function with no example has not demonstrated its behavior.

properties.description: Property-based invariants the function satisfies. Optional in the schema; a separate quality policy enforces presence for production records.

intent_tags.description: Semantic category tags for discovery. Drawn from a controlled vocabulary (TBD).

derived_from.description: Hashes of functions this one was derived from (specialized, generalized, composed). Lineage; not authoritative provenance.

supersedes.description: Hash of a function this one replaces. The superseded function is not deleted (content-addressing makes that impossible) but consumers may prefer this one.

body_hash.description: Hash of the expression body, stored separately from this metadata record. Allows discovery queries to scan metadata without pulling bodies. Resolvability is promised only for v0.2+ executable records: a v0.1 description-tier record whose source is outside the ingestion subset carries a SYNTHETIC body_hash (a fingerprint of the normalised source, so re-ingesting the same source reproduces the same record identity) that no stored artifact answers — consumers must treat an unresolvable body as 'this candidate cannot be run/certified here', never as store corruption.

content_address.description: Content-address: prefix + '_' + 64 lowercase hex chars (BLAKE3-256 digest). Prefix discriminates the kind of object. v0.1 fixes the algorithm; v0.2+ may introduce multihash.

intent_tag.description: Slash-separated semantic tag, e.g. 'transform/list', 'parse/integer'. Controlled vocabulary TBD.

type.description: v0.1: type written in the surface syntax as a string. v0.2+: structured type AST (separate schema). Required because untyped is not a valid state.

refinements.description: Predicates the type system alone cannot express. v0.1: predicate expressed as string. v0.2+: structured predicate AST.

effects.description: Effects the function exercises. Empty array means pure. Effects are closed-vocabulary in v0.1 — see effects enum.

capabilities.description: Capability tokens the function requires the caller to grant. Open-vocabulary; tokens use cap:path/segment form.

complexity.description: Big-O cost bound, e.g. 'O(n)', 'O(n log n)'. Optional in v0.1; required by future quality policy.

terminates.description: Termination guarantee. 'conditional' means termination depends on input shape; 'unknown' is for v0.1 records that have not been analyzed yet and is expected to become disallowed.

kind.description: pre: must hold on entry. post: must hold on exit. inv: must hold throughout.

expr.description: Predicate expression. v0.1: string in surface syntax. v0.2+: structured AST.

effect.description: Closed effect vocabulary for v0.1. Any new effect kind requires a schema version bump — keeps the vocabulary minimal (principle 7).

capability.description: Capability token, e.g. 'cap:fs/read/home', 'cap:net/connect/github.com'. Tokens are open-vocabulary so domains can declare their own resources.

args.description: Argument values in positional order. Any JSON value; the value-representation sub-language is TBD.

result.description: Result value. Any JSON value; the value-representation sub-language is TBD.

name.description: Identifier for the property (e.g. 'identity', 'composition'). Local to the function record; not globally unique.

expr.description: Property predicate. v0.1: string in surface syntax. v0.2+: structured AST evaluable by a property-based testing engine.
